meta.note: Corporate PAC federal contributions, 2024 cycle, rounded to the nearest $100,000. Curated snapshot for satire with a citation habit — verify against FEC/OpenSecrets before any public launch. Schema is feed-compatible for a future Integrity Index integration.

targets.name: Lockheed Martin PAC
targets.blurb: Builds the F-35, which is over budget. Your vote is not. It costs $0 and is worth $100.

targets.name: Boeing PAC
targets.blurb: Some doors optional. Your ballot's chain of custody is, mercifully, better regulated.

targets.name: RTX (Raytheon) PAC
targets.blurb: Precision-guided contributions to 400+ members of Congress. Guide yours to a polling place.

targets.name: Northrop Grumman PAC
targets.blurb: The B-21 is stealthy. So is influence, until you read the FEC filings.

targets.name: General Dynamics PAC
targets.blurb: Generally dynamic in appropriations season. Be specifically dynamic in November.

targets.name: Honeywell PAC
targets.blurb: Makes thermostats and one of the largest corporate PACs in America. Adjust the temperature.

targets.name: L3Harris PAC
targets.blurb: Communications systems for the battlefield. Your comms system is one oval and a pen.

targets.name: Pfizer PAC
targets.blurb: Develops vaccines. Also develops relationships with both parties, per the public record.

targets.name: AbbVie PAC
targets.blurb: Humira's patent had 130+ extensions. Your vote needs zero. It works the first time.

targets.name: Eli Lilly PAC
targets.blurb: Insulin prices came down after decades. Votes are price-controlled at exactly one each.

targets.name: Amgen PAC
targets.blurb: Biotech giant. The most effective compound ever synthesized remains voter turnout.

targets.name: Merck PAC
targets.blurb: Invented the term blockbuster drug. The ballot box is a generic — equally effective, free.

targets.name: Johnson & Johnson PAC
targets.blurb: No more tears, except at appropriations markup.

targets.name: PhRMA (trade association) PAC
targets.blurb: The industry's industry group. A PAC of PAC-havers. Cancel accordingly.

targets.name: ExxonMobil PAC
targets.blurb: Knew about climate models early. You can model an election: count the votes.

targets.name: Chevron PAC
targets.blurb: Human energy. Your energy: walk to a polling place, burn 40 calories, cancel $100.

targets.name: ConocoPhillips PAC
targets.blurb: Drills in the Arctic. You drill one oval.

targets.name: Marathon Petroleum PAC
targets.blurb: A marathon is 26.2 miles. Your polling place is closer than that. Probably.

targets.name: Valero PAC
targets.blurb: Refines crude into gasoline and contributions. Refine your grievance into a ballot.

targets.name: Occidental Petroleum PAC
targets.blurb: Occasionally occidental, consistently contributing.

targets.name: Koch Industries PAC
targets.blurb: The most famous political checkbook in America. It still can't fit inside a voting booth.

targets.name: Goldman Sachs PAC
targets.blurb: Does God's work, per its former CEO. Voting remains available to laypeople.

targets.name: Bank of America PAC
targets.blurb: Higher standards. Standard contribution limits. One standard vote cancels $100.

targets.name: JPMorgan Chase PAC
targets.blurb: Too big to fail. Your vote: too small to buy. That's the design.

targets.name: Wells Fargo PAC
targets.blurb: Once opened accounts nobody asked for. Open one nobody can close: a voter registration.

targets.name: Morgan Stanley PAC
targets.blurb: Manages $5 trillion in assets and one PAC. You manage one vote. Yours appreciates.

targets.name: Citigroup PAC
targets.blurb: Citi never sleeps. Polls close at 8. Set an alarm.

targets.name: BlackRock PAC
targets.blurb: Votes billions of shares in proxy season. In November, holds exactly zero proxies for you.

targets.name: American Express PAC
targets.blurb: Membership has its privileges. Citizenship has a better one.

targets.name: AT&T PAC
targets.blurb: Historically a top-3 corporate PAC. Can you hear the FEC filings now? Good.

targets.name: Comcast PAC
targets.blurb: Your appointment to cancel this influence is between 8am and 8pm on Election Day.

targets.name: Verizon PAC
targets.blurb: 5G coverage: most of America. Voting coverage: all of it, one per customer.

targets.name: T-Mobile PAC
targets.blurb: The un-carrier still carries a PAC. Un-carry $100 of it.

targets.name: Charter Communications PAC
targets.blurb: Spectrum: the service. Spectrum of influence: the FEC table below.

targets.name: Disney PAC
targets.blurb: The happiest PAC on earth. Your vote: the cheapest fast pass in democracy.

targets.name: Amazon PAC
targets.blurb: Same-day delivery on almost everything. Democracy still requires in-person or by-mail.

targets.name: Microsoft PAC
targets.blurb: Would you like to restart democracy now or schedule it for November 3?

targets.name: Google (Alphabet) PAC
targets.blurb: Knows what you searched. Doesn't know how you vote. Nobody does. That's the whole point.

targets.name: Meta PAC
targets.blurb: Moved fast, broke things. Voting: slow, unbroken since 1788, still shipping.

targets.name: Apple PAC*
targets.blurb: *Apple famously has no corporate PAC — this line item exists so we could tell you that. Executives give personally. The oval works anyway.

targets.name: Intuit PAC
targets.blurb: Lobbied for years to keep taxes complicated. Voting is still free-file.

targets.name: UnitedHealth Group PAC
targets.blurb: Your claim to one vote is pre-approved, no referral required.

targets.name: Blue Cross Blue Shield PAC
targets.blurb: In-network with most of Congress, per the filings. Your ballot has no deductible.

targets.name: Cigna PAC
targets.blurb: Prior authorization is not required to vote. We checked.

targets.name: Elevance Health PAC
targets.blurb: Formerly Anthem. Rebrands cost millions; your re-registration after a move costs $0.

targets.name: Aflac PAC
targets.blurb: The duck is charming. The disbursement table is less so.

targets.name: State Farm PAC
targets.blurb: Like a good neighbor, show up to the same polling place as your actual neighbors.

targets.name: Archer Daniels Midland PAC
targets.blurb: In everything you eat. Not in your ballot. Keep it that way.

targets.name: Tyson Foods PAC
targets.blurb: Processes 45 million chickens a week. Your vote is processed once, by hand, with witnesses.

targets.name: American Crystal Sugar PAC
targets.blurb: A beet co-op that routinely out-gives Wall Street banks. Big Sugar is real and it is filing.

targets.name: John Deere PAC
targets.blurb: Fought right-to-repair. Your democracy ships with user-serviceable parts: you.

targets.name: Coca-Cola PAC
targets.blurb: Teaches the world to sing. The chorus that matters is counted in November.

targets.name: McDonald's PAC
targets.blurb: Billions and billions served. Ballots: one per person, no supersizing.

targets.name: Union Pacific PAC
targets.blurb: Owns 32,000 miles of track. You need to travel, on average, two.

targets.name: UPS PAC
targets.blurb: What can brown do for you? $2.5M for Congress, apparently. Return to sender.

targets.name: FedEx PAC
targets.blurb: When your vote absolutely, positively has to be there by 8pm on November 3.

targets.name: Delta Air Lines PAC
targets.blurb: Boarding by zones. Voting: everyone is Zone 1.

targets.name: American Airlines PAC
targets.blurb: Your vote has no change fees, no blackout dates, and the middle seat is a private booth.

targets.name: General Motors PAC
targets.blurb: Sees the USA in a Chevrolet. The USA would like to see you at your precinct.

targets.name: Altria PAC
targets.blurb: Rebranded from Philip Morris. The filings did not get the rebrand memo.

targets.name: Reynolds American PAC
targets.blurb: Warning: contains influence. Voting has no known side effects except results.

targets.name: Anheuser-Busch PAC
targets.blurb: This vote's for you. Literally. It's the only one you get.

targets.name: Beer Wholesalers (NBWA) PAC
targets.blurb: The beer distributors' PAC out-gives almost every corporation in America. Big Beer runs deep.

targets.name: Caesars Entertainment PAC
targets.blurb: The house always wins, except in a secret ballot, where the house isn't registered.

targets.name: Fairshake (crypto super PAC)
targets.blurb: Not a corporate PAC — a $169M industry super PAC war chest (reported independent expenditures). The final boss of this ledger. Bring friends.

targets.name: Coinbase PAC
targets.blurb: Your vote is proof-of-personhood. One per wallet. No gas fees.
